title: grevillea tripartita subsp. macrostylis
intro: Grevillea tripartita subsp. macrostylis, also known as the Western Spider Flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae. It is native to Western Australia and is a shrub-like plant with red or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, and is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. The herbal preparations of Grevillea tripartita subsp. macrostylis typically involve infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to make a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
